mechanism_narrative: Persistently near-zero FEDFUNDS levels sustained over multiple quarters keep the equity risk premium compressed relative to bond yields, producing a structural tailwind for S&P 500 returns over a full quarter as investors reach for yield in equities.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% as of 2014-10-01, unchanged for years
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and 60-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or returns during near-zero rate periods are not statistically different from full-sample average returns (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE declines by more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 6-month window while remaining above the natural rate estimate, it signals improving labor market slack absorption without yet triggering monetary tightening fears, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE fell from 6.1 in June 2014 to 5.7 (revised) by October 2014
failure_condition: In more than 40% of trigger instances, 20-day forward returns are negative, or the average triggered-period return is not significantly higher than the unconditional 20-day mean (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A joint acceleration in INDPRO month-over-month growth combined with rising RSAFS retail sales signals synchronized supply and demand expansion, producing a short-horizon positive momentum burst in S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as growth data beats embedded expectations.
candidate_trigger: both INDPRO and RSAFS month-over-month growth exceed their trailing 12-month average simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage 105.07 vs revised 103.06 in Sep 2014, RSAFS 444491 vs revised 431903 in Oct 2014
failure_condition: Joint-trigger periods produce positive 5-day forward returns in less than 55% of instances, or the interaction term adds no incremental Rank IC over either single-series model (delta IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: During a post-housing-crash recovery regime, sustained increases in HOUST housing starts above pre-recovery baseline levels signal durable consumer balance-sheet repair and confidence, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon; this relationship is expected to be much weaker or absent outside such a recovery regime.
candidate_trigger: HOUST trailing 12-month level above pre-2011 recovery-regime baseline
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1017-1023k units in Sep 2014, well above 2009-2011 trough of ~500k
failure_condition: Rank IC between HOUST trend and 60-day forward returns is not materially higher (delta < 0.02) inside the recovery regime versus other regimes, falsifying the regime-conditional claim.

mechanism_narrative: A steady positive trend in PAYEMS nonfarm payroll growth, independent of threshold or regime, reflects ongoing income and consumption support that translates into persistently positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS rose from 139680k to revised 139798k in Oct 2014
failure_condition: Rank IC between PAYEMS month-over-month change and 20-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation crosses above 2.5%, unexpectedly high inflation readings raise market expectations of imminent monetary tightening, causing a short-horizon negative reaction in S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days as discount rate expectations rise.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 237.48-237.63 in Sep 2014, YoY inflation near 1.7%, below trigger threshold
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following CPI threshold breaches are positive in more than 55% of instances, or the effect is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) over a 3-year rolling sample.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward trend in INDPRO industrial production over a trailing quarter reflects expanding real economic capacity utilization that supports corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon regardless of prevailing regime.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO vintage value 105.07 as of Sep 2014, trending upward from prior quarters
failure_condition: Rank IC between trailing INDPRO trend and 60-day forward SPX returns is below 0.02 across two consecutive non-overlapping 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS remains near the zero lower bound while UNRATE simultaneously falls below 6%, the combination signals that monetary policy is lagging behind labor market improvement, creating a 'catch-up tightening' anticipation effect that dampens S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as markets price in accelerated future rate hikes.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.09% and UNRATE at 5.7-5.8% as of Oct 2014
failure_condition: Joint-trigger periods show positive or neutral 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or the interaction model's Rank IC does not exceed the best single-series model by at least 0.01.
